<<Economic Stress Analysis>>
The maximum incidents occur at an economic stress index of 2, yielding 13 incidents per 1000 people.
To keep incidents below 20, the community must maintain an economic stress index between 0 and approximately 3.42.
This calculation ensures human safety while avoiding the peak of the curve.
Just as a baseball player aims for a specific strike zone, policymakers must target this range.
Water resources and western economic models often influence these stress factors indirectly.
Maintaining this balance is crucial for long-term community health.
